title: Single-Cell Multiple Displacement Amplification (SCMDA)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cmfnf","text":"Single-Cell Multiple Displacement Amplification (SCMDA) is a protocol developed for accurate single-nucleotide variant detection in single cells after whole-genome amplification. This protocol accompanies Dong et al (Nature Methods, publishing online 20 March, 2017; 10.038/nmeth.4227); it was added to the manuscript after formal peer review, as an aid to users","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Isolate single cells (multiple single cell isolation methods are available, for example, the CellRaft system, which was used here) and deposit into 0.2-ml PCR tubes containing 2.5 µl PBS.
Immediately place the tubes on dry ice and store at -80°C.
Carry out the following Step 1) to 4) in a PCR workstation.
Add 1 µl exo-resistant random primers and 3 µl lysis buffer containing 400 mM KOH, 100 mM DTT, 10 mM EDTA solution, into the PCR tube containing a single cell.
Flick the mixture several times, spin down quickly and keep it on ice for 10 min.
Add 3 µl stop buffer containing 400 mM HCl and 600 mM Tris-HCl solution (1 M, pH7.5) to neutralize the lysis buffer. Keep it on ice for 2 min.
Add 32 µl of master mix containing 30 µl MDA reaction buffer and 2 µl Phi29 polymerase. Mix by pipetting gently.
Incubate at 30°C for 1.5 hr and 65°C for 3 min.
Purify amplified product with 75.6 µl AMPureXP-beads and mix it by pipetting.
Leave the mixture at room temperature for 5 min and put it on a magnetic separator until the solution is clear.
Remove the clear liquid by pipetting and wash beads twice with fresh 80% ethanol.
Open lid of the tube to air-dry beads for about 5 min. (Avoid beads cracking)
Take out the tube from magnetic separator and elute beads using 32 µl nuclease-free water.
Put back the tube to magnetic separator and keep it at room temperature for 5 min until the solution is clear and a pellet is formed again.
Transfer 30 µl clear liquid from the tube to a clean tube.
Store the purified product at 4°C for short-term storage or -20°C for long-term storage.
Dilute the purified product 10-20 times and quantify the samples using Qubit. Usually, the yield of the purified product is 10-12 µg.

Check the product size by agarose gel electrophoresis. The following conditions are recommended: 1.0% agarose gel; 1.0% TBE solution; 100V for 40 min. A bright band (with a little smear) over 10 kb in a gel picture is desirable.
Dilute the purified product and unamplified genomic DNA as a control to 1 ng/µl using nuclease-free water.
Prepare 96-well plate and the PCR mixture for each well including 5 µl Fast Sybr green master mix, 1 µl locus-specific primers, 2 µl nuclease-free water and 2 µl diluted DNA.
Seal the plate and spin down.
Incubate under the following qPCR program: 95°C for 20s, thermocycling (40 cycles) at 95°C for 3 s, and 60°C for 30 s.
Calculate the relative uniformity values (RUVs) for 8 loci (equation (1) in Supplementary Notes, Dong et al., Nature Methods in press).
Select the samples with RUV values for at least 6 of the 8 loci of between 0.25-4 for library construction.
X.D., L.Z., M.L., A.M. and J.V. are co-founders of SingulOmics Corp.

Accurate identification of single-nucleotide variants in whole-genome-amplified single cells by Xiao Dong, Lei Zhang, Brandon Milholland, Moonsook Lee, Alexander Y Maslov, Tao Wang, and Jan Vijg published in Nature Methods on 20 Mar, 2017 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.4227
creator: Protocols Importer
